Explicit Interface Implementations

Add(object, object)

Memoizes an object uniquely associated with the specified key. Adding more objects than allowed (as indicated by the .MaximumSize property) would cause one or more previously memoized items to be removed based on an implementation-specific eviction strategy.

virtual void Add(object vtKey, object ppObject)

Parameters

vtKey object
ppObject object

Examples

Sample Snippet

CacheEnabled

Indicates whether disk-based caching is enabled.

virtual bool CacheEnabled { get; set; }

Returns

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

Clear()

Clears the object cache associated with the memoizer.

virtual void Clear()

Examples

Sample Snippet

Count

The number of object currently held by the memoizer.

virtual int Count { get; }

Returns

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

Exists(object)

Returns TRUE if a previously memoized object associated with the specified key is found.

virtual bool Exists(object vtKey)

Parameters

vtKey object

Returns

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

FolderPath

The path to the parent folder for storing cached objects.

virtual string FolderPath { get; set; }

Returns

string

Examples

Sample Snippet

Get(object)

Retrieves the previously memoized object associated with the specified key and increments its usage count.

virtual object Get(object vtKey)

Parameters

vtKey object

Returns

object
